title: includes()
subtitle: Check whether an element exists
goal: Learn to check whether an element exists in an array using includes().
explanation: includes() checks whether an array contains a value.
If the value is found, it returns true.
If the value is absent, it returns false.
The method does not return the element's position.
includes() is very convenient for quick checks.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: You look in the refrigerator to check whether there is any milk. There are only two possible answers: yes or no.

remember: It is used to check whether a value exists.
It does not return the element's index.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners sometimes expect to get the element's position. Use indexOf() for that.
